description: A feedback collection system with an embeddable website widget for screenshot capture, screen recording with audio narration, and annotation. Features a central dashboard for processing feedback with AI-powered triage, ticket drafting, and export to Linear, Notion, or JSON (prd.json format for AI workflows). Open source with self-host option, or use hosted version with seat-based pricing.

userStories.title: Set up project foundation with Next.js 15 and Convex
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 15 project initialized with App Router
Tailwind CSS configured with custom theme colors from design
userStories.notes: Use the retro design system colors: retro-paper (#F7F5F0), retro-black (#1a1a1a), retro-yellow (#F3C952), retro-red (#E85D52), retro-blue (#6B9AC4), retro-lavender (#D4C4E8), retro-peach (#F4A261)

userStories.title: Create Convex database schema for all entities
acceptanceCriteria: users table with id, email, name, avatar, createdAt
teams table with id, name, slug, createdAt
teamMembers table with userId, teamId, role (admin/member), joinedAt
projects table with id, teamId, name, description, settings, createdAt
widgets table with id, projectId, widgetKey, siteUrl, config, createdAt
feedback table with full schema (see notes)
All supporting tables created (aiAnalysis, conversations, comments, etc.)
Appropriate indexes created for common queries
userStories.notes: feedback table: widgetId, projectId, teamId, type (bug/feature), title, description, screenshotUrl, recordingUrl, recordingDuration, status (new/triaging/drafted/exported/resolved), priority (low/medium/high/critical), tags[], submitterEmail?, submitterName?, assigneeId?, metadata {browser, os, url, timestamp}, createdAt. Also create: aiAnalysis, conversations, comments, activityLog, widgetConfig, exports, automationRules, integrations, storageConfig, apiKeys, usageTracking, subscriptions, notifications, webhookLogs, exportTemplates

userStories.title: Implement Clerk authentication integration
acceptanceCriteria: Sign up and sign in flows working
User data syncs to Convex users table on auth
Session tokens passed to Convex for auth
userStories.notes: Set up ClerkProvider, middleware.ts, and user sync webhook/mutation

userStories.title: Build team creation and management
acceptanceCriteria: createTeam mutation creates team and adds creator as admin
acceptInvite mutation adds user as member
updateMemberRole mutation for admins to change roles
userStories.notes: Two roles only: admin (full access, billing, invites) and member (view/process feedback)

userStories.title: Build landing page from design mockup
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section with headline, subtext, and CTAs
Open source section with self-host messaging
Matches retro design aesthetic from mockup
userStories.notes: Use provided HTML mockup as reference. Implement with Next.js and Tailwind, converting to React components.

userStories.title: Create dashboard layout and navigation
acceptanceCriteria: User footer with profile and settings
userStories.notes: Use provided dashboard HTML mockup as reference

userStories.title: Build project and widget management
acceptanceCriteria: getProjects query returns team's projects with feedback counts
updateWidget mutation for site URL and config
Widget key can be regenerated (invalidates old key)
userStories.notes: Widget key is public (embedded in sites), used to route feedback to correct project

userStories.title: Build embeddable widget script - core structure
acceptanceCriteria: Widget script builds to single JS file (<50KB minified)
Script initializes with widget key from data attribute
Floating button appears in configurable position
Modal has screenshot and record buttons
Works across modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
userStories.notes: Build with vanilla JS/TS, bundle with esbuild or similar. Widget config: position, colors, button text

userStories.title: Implement screenshot capture in widget
acceptanceCriteria: Uses html2canvas or native API based on browser support
User can annotate with drawing tools (pen, highlighter)
userStories.notes: Keep annotation tools simple: pen (red), highlighter (yellow), shapes (circle, arrow). Canvas-based drawing.

userStories.title: Implement screen recording with audio in widget
acceptanceCriteria: Record button requests screen + audio permissions
Uses getDisplayMedia API for screen capture
userStories.notes: Use MediaRecorder API. Format: webm with vp9 codec. Show clear recording indicator so users know it's active.

userStories.title: Build widget submission form and offline queue
acceptanceCriteria: After capture, form shows with title input
Type selector: Bug or Feature Request
Queue retries on next page load or connectivity
userStories.notes: Offline queue ensures no feedback is lost. Retry with exponential backoff.

userStories.title: Create widget API endpoint with rate limiting
acceptanceCriteria: Validates widget key exists and is active
Rate limiting: 10 submissions per minute per IP
Rate limiting: 100 submissions per day per widget
Honeypot field for basic spam detection
Videos uploaded to configured external storage
userStories.notes: Use upstash/ratelimit or similar for rate limiting. Return appropriate error codes for rate limits.

userStories.title: Build feedback inbox view
acceptanceCriteria: Lists all feedback for selected project
Each item shows: title, type badge, priority, timestamp, thumbnail
Click selects feedback and opens detail panel
Real-time updates when new feedback arrives
userStories.notes: Match the inbox design from mockup. Use Convex real-time subscriptions.

userStories.title: Build feedback detail view
acceptanceCriteria: Shows full feedback details in right panel
Metadata shown: browser, OS, URL, timestamp
userStories.notes: Match the detail panel design from mockup

userStories.title: Implement comments and activity log
acceptanceCriteria: Comment input at bottom of detail panel
Each comment shows author, content, timestamp
Activity log shows status changes, assignments, exports
Activity entries show who, what, when
Comments and activity interleaved or tabbed
userStories.notes: Activity log is automatic (system-generated), comments are user-written

userStories.title: Build full-text search
acceptanceCriteria: Filters can be combined with search
userStories.notes: Use Convex search indexes. Consider adding search across tags and submitter info.

userStories.title: Implement API key management for AI providers
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page section for AI configuration
Keys only decrypted server-side for API calls
userStories.notes: Use encryption at rest. Never expose keys to client. Show masked key after saving.

userStories.title: Implement AI auto-categorization
acceptanceCriteria: When feedback is received, AI analyzes content
Suggests type (bug vs feature) with confidence
Suggestions shown in UI with accept/reject
Works with both OpenAI and Anthropic
userStories.notes: Run as background job after feedback received. Use vision API to analyze screenshots.

userStories.title: Implement AI solution suggestions
acceptanceCriteria: AI analyzes feedback and suggests potential fixes
For bugs: suggests investigation steps, potential causes
Suggestions shown in expandable section in detail view
Suggestions can be copied or added to ticket draft
Works with both OpenAI and Anthropic
userStories.notes: This is the 'rubber ducky' feature - AI that might solve issues before they become tickets

userStories.title: Implement AI ticket drafting (priority feature)
acceptanceCriteria: Draft Ticket button in feedback detail
AI generates formatted ticket from feedback
Ticket includes: clear title, description, repro steps, expected vs actual
For features: includes user story format
Works with both OpenAI and Anthropic
userStories.notes: This is the highest priority AI feature. Output should be clean, actionable tickets ready for Linear/Notion.

userStories.title: Implement AI conversation
acceptanceCriteria: Chat interface in feedback detail panel
User can ask AI questions about the feedback
AI has context of screenshot, description, metadata
Responses can be copied to comments or ticket draft
Works with both OpenAI and Anthropic
userStories.notes: Conversation helps explore the issue deeper before deciding on action

userStories.title: Build Linear integration
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page for Linear API key/OAuth
Select default Linear team and project
Export to Linear button in feedback detail
Creates Linear issue with title, description, labels
Attaches screenshot/video as link or upload
userStories.notes: Use Linear SDK. Support both OAuth and API key auth.

userStories.title: Build Notion integration
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page for Notion OAuth connection
Select default Notion database for tickets
Export to Notion button in feedback detail
Creates Notion page with properties mapped
userStories.notes: Use Notion SDK. Require user to share specific database with integration.

userStories.title: Build JSON export in prd.json format
acceptanceCriteria: Export as JSON button for single feedback
Bulk export for multiple selected items
JSON format matches prd.json userStories structure
Includes: id, title, acceptanceCriteria (AI-generated), priority, notes
Export all project feedback as full prd.json
Download as file or copy to clipboard
userStories.notes: Format for AI dev workflows like Ralph. AI generates acceptance criteria from feedback.

userStories.title: Implement webhook system
acceptanceCriteria: Webhooks POST JSON payload with HMAC signature
Retry failed webhooks (3 attempts with backoff)
Test webhook button sends sample payload
userStories.notes: Use Convex scheduled functions for retries. Log all attempts.

userStories.title: Build REST API for external access
userStories.notes: RESTful API for custom integrations. Include pagination, filtering, sorting.

userStories.title: Implement automation rules
acceptanceCriteria: Automation rules section in project settings
Create rule with: trigger, conditions, action
Conditions: type equals, priority equals, tags contain
Actions: export to Linear, export to Notion, send webhook, assign to user
userStories.notes: Example: 'All bugs with priority Critical -> Export to Linear automatically'

userStories.title: Build analytics dashboard
acceptanceCriteria: Breakdown by type (bugs vs features pie chart)
Breakdown by status (funnel or bar chart)
Top tags word cloud or bar chart
userStories.notes: Keep charts simple. Use recharts or similar. Focus on actionable insights.

userStories.title: Implement email notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Email sent on: new feedback, assignment, comment mention
Emails include feedback summary and link to dashboard
Can set frequency: instant, daily digest, weekly digest
userStories.notes: Use Resend for transactional email. Design emails to match brand.

userStories.title: Implement in-app notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Notifications for: new feedback, assignments, comments, exports
Click notification navigates to relevant feedback
userStories.notes: Keep in sync with email notifications. Same events, different channel.

userStories.title: Build submitter status page
acceptanceCriteria: Requires magic link sent to submitter email
Shows feedback status (new, in progress, resolved)
Does NOT show internal comments or team info
Optional: submitter notified when status changes
userStories.notes: Only accessible if submitter provided email. Magic link for security.

userStories.title: Implement GDPR compliance features
acceptanceCriteria: Data export for submitters (their feedback data as JSON)
Deletion removes submitter PII but keeps anonymized feedback
Admin can export all team data
Admin can delete entire project/team data
Cookie consent if needed for widget
userStories.notes: GDPR compliance is required for EU users. Keep it straightforward.

userStories.title: Set up Stripe integration for billing
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe configured with products and prices
Pro tier: per-seat pricing, unlimited feedback
subscriptions table tracks team subscription status
userStories.notes: Use Stripe Checkout and Customer Portal. Test mode only initially.

userStories.title: Implement usage tracking and limits
acceptanceCriteria: Track feedback count per team per month
Show usage in dashboard (e.g., '18/25 this month')
Pro tier has no feedback limit
Seat count enforced (can't add members beyond seats)
userStories.notes: Soft limit approach: allow submission but show upgrade prompts, vs hard block

userStories.title: Build pricing page and upgrade flow
acceptanceCriteria: Pricing page showing Free vs Pro comparison
Current plan indicator for logged-in users
userStories.notes: Keep pricing simple. Emphasize self-host as alternative to Pro.

userStories.title: Build widget customization UI
acceptanceCriteria: Widget settings page in project settings
Color picker for button and modal colors
userStories.notes: Allow brand customization while maintaining usability

userStories.title: Create widget installation documentation
acceptanceCriteria: Copy-paste script tag with widget key
Inline code snippets in dashboard for quick copy
userStories.notes: Make installation as simple as adding Google Analytics

userStories.title: Build configurable external storage
acceptanceCriteria: Support S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, R2, MinIO)
Fallback to Convex if not configured
userStories.notes: Critical for self-hosters who want full data control

userStories.title: Create self-hosting documentation
acceptanceCriteria: Self-host guide in docs or README
Convex self-host or cloud options explained
userStories.notes: n8n-style self-hosting: truly open, no artificial limits

userStories.title: Build team settings page
userStories.notes: Admins can do everything except delete team. That requires owner.

userStories.title: Build user profile and settings
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page with name, email, avatar
API key management for personal use
userStories.notes: Keep user settings separate from team settings

userStories.title: Implement export templates
acceptanceCriteria: Default templates for Linear, Notion, JSON
userStories.notes: Use Handlebars or similar for templating. Allow custom formatting.

userStories.title: Write unit tests for core functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Tests for Convex mutations and queries
userStories.notes: Use Vitest. Focus on business logic, not UI.

userStories.title: Write E2E tests for critical flows
acceptanceCriteria: Test: Sign up and create team
Test: View and process feedback in dashboard
userStories.notes: Use Playwright. Focus on happy paths for critical user journeys.

userStories.title: Performance optimization and polish
acceptanceCriteria: Loading states for all async operations
Error boundaries with friendly error pages
userStories.notes: Final polish pass before launch

designNotes: FeedbackFlow is a feedback collection system that turns screenshots and recordings into actionable tickets. The widget is lightweight and lives on user sites. The dashboard is where teams process feedback with AI assistance. Export to Linear, Notion, or JSON (prd.json format for AI workflows). Open source with self-host option, or use hosted version with seat-based pricing (Free: 1 seat, 25 feedback/month; Pro: per-seat, unlimited). Design uses retro aesthetic with paper texture, bold borders, and shadow effects.
